```rust
490	    pub fn input(&mut self, event: KeyEvent) {
491	        // Only process key presses or repeats; ignore releases to avoid inserting
492	        // characters on key-up events when modifiers are no longer reported.
493	        if !matches!(event.kind, KeyEventKind::Press | KeyEventKind::Repeat) {

498	        } else {

501	        }
```

#### codex-rs/tui/src/public_widgets/composer_input.rs — input(method)

```rust
1	//! Public wrapper around the internal ChatComposer for simple, reusable text input.
2	//!
3	//! This exposes a minimal interface suitable for other crates (e.g.,
4	//! codex-cloud-tasks) to reuse the mature composer behavior: multi-line input,
5	//! paste heuristics, Enter-to-submit, and Shift+Enter for newline.
6	
7	use crossterm::event::KeyEvent;
8	use ratatui::buffer::Buffer;
9	use ratatui::layout::Rect;
10	use std::time::Duration;
11	
12	use crate::app_event::AppEvent;
13	use crate::app_event_sender::AppEventSender;
14	use crate::bottom_pane::ChatComposer;
15	use crate::bottom_pane::InputResult;
16	use crate::render::renderable::Renderable;
17	
18	/// Action returned from feeding a key event into the ComposerInput.
19	pub enum ComposerAction {
20	    /// The user submitted the current text (typically via Enter). Contains the submitted text.
21	    Submitted(String),
22	    /// No submission occurred; UI may need to redraw if `needs_redraw()` returned true.
23	    None,
24	}
25	
26	/// A minimal, public wrapper for the internal `ChatComposer` that behaves as a
27	/// reusable text input field with submit semantics.
28	pub struct ComposerInput {
29	    inner: ChatComposer,
30	    _tx: tokio::sync::mpsc::UnboundedSender<AppEvent>,
31	    rx: tokio::sync::mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<AppEvent>,
32	}
33	
34	impl ComposerInput {
35	    /// Create a new composer input with a neutral placeholder.
36	    pub fn new() -> Self {
37	        let (tx, rx) = tokio::sync::mpsc::unbounded_channel();
38	        let sender = AppEventSender::new(tx.clone());
39	        // `enhanced_keys_supported=true` enables Shift+Enter newline hint/behavior.
40	        let inner = ChatComposer::new(
41	            /*has_input_focus*/ true,
42	            sender,
43	            /*enhanced_keys_supported*/ true,
44	            "Compose new task".to_string(),
45	            /*disable_paste_burst*/ false,
46	        );
47	        Self { inner, _tx: tx, rx }
48	    }
49	
50	    /// Returns true if the input is empty.
51	    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
52	        self.inner.is_empty()
53	    }
54	
55	    /// Clear the input text.
56	    pub fn clear(&mut self) {
57	        self.inner
58	            .set_text_content(String::new(), Vec::new(), Vec::new());
59	    }
60	
61	    /// Feed a key event into the composer and return a high-level action.
62	    pub fn input(&mut self, key: KeyEvent) -> ComposerAction {
63	        let action = match self.inner.handle_key_event(key).0 {
64	            InputResult::Submitted { text, .. } => ComposerAction::Submitted(text),
65	            _ => ComposerAction::None,
66	        };
67	        self.drain_app_events();
68	        action
69	    }
70	
71	    pub fn handle_paste(&mut self, pasted: String) -> bool {
72	        let handled = self.inner.handle_paste(pasted);
73	        self.drain_app_events();
74	        handled
75	    }
76	
77	    /// Override the footer hint items displayed under the composer.
78	    /// Each tuple is rendered as "<key> <label>", with keys styled.
79	    pub fn set_hint_items(&mut self, items: Vec<(impl Into<String>, impl Into<String>)>) {
80	        let mapped: Vec<(String, String)> = items
81	            .into_iter()
82	            .map(|(k, v)| (k.into(), v.into()))
83	            .collect();
84	        self.inner.set_footer_hint_override(Some(mapped));
85	    }
86	
87	    /// Clear any previously set custom hint items and restore the default hints.
88	    pub fn clear_hint_items(&mut self) {
89	        self.inner.set_footer_hint_override(/*items*/ None);
90	    }
91	
92	    /// Desired height (in rows) for a given width.
93	    pub fn desired_height(&self, width: u16) -> u16 {
94	        self.inner.desired_height(width)
95	    }
96	
97	    /// Compute the on-screen cursor position for the given area.
98	    pub fn cursor_pos(&self, area: Rect) -> Option<(u16, u16)> {
99	        self.inner.cursor_pos(area)
100	    }
101	
102	    /// Render the input into the provided buffer at `area`.
103	    pub fn render_ref(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
104	        self.inner.render(area, buf);
105	    }
106	
107	    /// Return true if a paste-burst detection is currently active.
108	    pub fn is_in_paste_burst(&self) -> bool {
109	        self.inner.is_in_paste_burst()
110	    }
111	
112	    /// Flush a pending paste-burst if the inter-key timeout has elapsed.
113	    /// Returns true if text changed and a redraw is warranted.
114	    pub fn flush_paste_burst_if_due(&mut self) -> bool {
115	        let flushed = self.inner.flush_paste_burst_if_due();
116	        self.drain_app_events();
117	        flushed
118	    }
119	
120	    /// Recommended delay to schedule the next micro-flush frame while a
121	    /// paste-burst is active.
122	    pub fn recommended_flush_delay() -> Duration {
123	        crate::bottom_pane::ChatComposer::recommended_paste_flush_delay()
124	    }
125	
126	    fn drain_app_events(&mut self) {
127	        while self.rx.try_recv().is_ok() {}
128	    }
129	}
130	
131	impl Default for ComposerInput {
132	    fn default() -> Self {
133	        Self::new()
134	    }
135	}
```

```rust
69	    }
70	
71	    /// normalized creates a `Constrained` value with a normalizer function and a validator that allows any value.
72	    pub fn normalized(
73	        initial_value: T,
74	        normalizer: impl Fn(T) -> T + Send + Sync + 'static,
75	    ) -> ConstraintResult<Self> {
76	        let validator: Arc<ConstraintValidator<T>> = Arc::new(|_| Ok(()));
77	        let normalizer: Arc<ConstraintNormalizer<T>> = Arc::new(normalizer);
78	        let normalized = normalizer(initial_value);
79	        validator(&normalized)?;
80	        Ok(Self {
81	            value: normalized,
82	            validator,
83	            normalizer: Some(normalizer),
84	        })
85	    }
86	
87	    pub fn allow_any(initial_value: T) -> Self {
88	        Self {
```

```rust
435	    async fn prompt(
436	        &self,
437	        program: &AbsolutePathBuf,
438	        argv: &[String],
439	        workdir: &AbsolutePathBuf,
440	        stopwatch: &Stopwatch,
441	        additional_permissions: Option<AdditionalPermissionProfile>,
442	    ) -> anyhow::Result<PromptDecision> {
443	        let command = join_program_and_argv(program, argv);
444	        let workdir = workdir.clone();
445	        let session = self.session.clone();
446	        let turn = self.turn.clone();
447	        let call_id = self.call_id.clone();
448	        let approval_id = Some(Uuid::new_v4().to_string());
449	        let environment_id = Some(self.environment_id.clone());
450	        let source = self.tool_name;
451	        let guardian_review_id = routes_approval_to_guardian(&turn).then(new_guardian_review_id);
452	        Ok(stopwatch
453	            .pause_for(async move {
454	                // 1) Run PermissionRequest hooks
455	                let permission_request = PermissionRequestPayload::bash(
456	                    codex_shell_command::parse_command::shlex_join(&command),
457	                    /*description*/ None,
458	                );
459	                let effective_approval_id = approval_id.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| call_id.clone());
460	                match run_permission_request_hooks(
461	                    &session,
462	                    &turn,
463	                    &effective_approval_id,
464	                    permission_request,
465	                )
466	                .await
467	                {
468	                    Some(PermissionRequestDecision::Allow) => {
469	                        return PromptDecision {
470	                            decision: ReviewDecision::Approved,
471	                            guardian_review_id: None,
472	                            rejection_message: None,
473	                        };
474	                    }
475	                    Some(PermissionRequestDecision::Deny { message }) => {
476	                        return PromptDecision {
477	                            decision: ReviewDecision::Denied,
478	                            guardian_review_id: None,
479	                            rejection_message: Some(message),
480	                        };
481	                    }
482	                    None => {}
483	                }
484	
485	                // 2) Route to Guardian if configured
486	                if let Some(review_id) = guardian_review_id.clone() {
487	                    let decision = review_approval_request(
488	                        &session,
489	                        &turn,
490	                        review_id.clone(),
491	                        GuardianApprovalRequest::Execve {
492	                            id: call_id.clone(),
493	                            source,
494	                            program: program.to_string_lossy().into_owned(),
495	                            argv: argv.to_vec(),
496	                            cwd: workdir.clone(),
497	                            additional_permissions,
498	                        },
499	                        /*retry_reason*/ None,
500	                    )
501	                    .await;
502	                    return PromptDecision {
503	                        decision,
504	                        guardian_review_id,
505	                        rejection_message: None,
506	                    };
507	                }
508	
509	                // 3) Fall back to regular user prompt
510	                let decision = session
511	                    .request_command_approval(
512	                        &turn,
513	                        call_id,
514	                        approval_id,
515	                        environment_id,
516	                        command,
517	                        workdir.clone(),
518	                        /*reason*/ None,
519	                        /*network_approval_context*/ None,
520	                        /*proposed_execpolicy_amendment*/ None,
521	                        additional_permissions,
522	                        Some(vec![ReviewDecision::Approved, ReviewDecision::Abort]),
523	                    )
524	                    .await;
525	                PromptDecision {
526	                    decision,
527	                    guardian_review_id: None,
528	                    rejection_message: None,
529	                }
530	            })
531	            .await)
532	    }
533	
534	    #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
535	    async fn process_decision(
```

#### codex-rs/tui/src/public_widgets/composer_input.rs — input(method)

```rust
1	//! Public wrapper around the internal ChatComposer for simple, reusable text input.
2	//!
3	//! This exposes a minimal interface suitable for other crates (e.g.,
4	//! codex-cloud-tasks) to reuse the mature composer behavior: multi-line input,
5	//! paste heuristics, Enter-to-submit, and Shift+Enter for newline.
6	
7	use crossterm::event::KeyEvent;
8	use ratatui::buffer::Buffer;
9	use ratatui::layout::Rect;
10	use std::time::Duration;
11	
12	use crate::app_event::AppEvent;
13	use crate::app_event_sender::AppEventSender;
14	use crate::bottom_pane::ChatComposer;
15	use crate::bottom_pane::InputResult;
16	use crate::render::renderable::Renderable;
17	
18	/// Action returned from feeding a key event into the ComposerInput.
19	pub enum ComposerAction {
20	    /// The user submitted the current text (typically via Enter). Contains the submitted text.
21	    Submitted(String),
22	    /// No submission occurred; UI may need to redraw if `needs_redraw()` returned true.
23	    None,
24	}
25	
26	/// A minimal, public wrapper for the internal `ChatComposer` that behaves as a
27	/// reusable text input field with submit semantics.
28	pub struct ComposerInput {
29	    inner: ChatComposer,
30	    _tx: tokio::sync::mpsc::UnboundedSender<AppEvent>,
31	    rx: tokio::sync::mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<AppEvent>,
32	}
33	
34	impl ComposerInput {
35	    /// Create a new composer input with a neutral placeholder.
36	    pub fn new() -> Self {
37	        let (tx, rx) = tokio::sync::mpsc::unbounded_channel();
38	        let sender = AppEventSender::new(tx.clone());
39	        // `enhanced_keys_supported=true` enables Shift+Enter newline hint/behavior.
40	        let inner = ChatComposer::new(
41	            /*has_input_focus*/ true,
42	            sender,
43	            /*enhanced_keys_supported*/ true,
44	            "Compose new task".to_string(),
45	            /*disable_paste_burst*/ false,
46	        );
47	        Self { inner, _tx: tx, rx }
48	    }
49	
50	    /// Returns true if the input is empty.
51	    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
52	        self.inner.is_empty()
53	    }
54	
55	    /// Clear the input text.
56	    pub fn clear(&mut self) {
57	        self.inner
58	            .set_text_content(String::new(), Vec::new(), Vec::new());
59	    }
60	
61	    /// Feed a key event into the composer and return a high-level action.
62	    pub fn input(&mut self, key: KeyEvent) -> ComposerAction {
63	        let action = match self.inner.handle_key_event(key).0 {
64	            InputResult::Submitted { text, .. } => ComposerAction::Submitted(text),
65	            _ => ComposerAction::None,
66	        };
67	        self.drain_app_events();
68	        action
69	    }
70	
71	    pub fn handle_paste(&mut self, pasted: String) -> bool {
72	        let handled = self.inner.handle_paste(pasted);
73	        self.drain_app_events();
74	        handled
75	    }
76	
77	    /// Override the footer hint items displayed under the composer.
78	    /// Each tuple is rendered as "<key> <label>", with keys styled.
79	    pub fn set_hint_items(&mut self, items: Vec<(impl Into<String>, impl Into<String>)>) {
80	        let mapped: Vec<(String, String)> = items
81	            .into_iter()
82	            .map(|(k, v)| (k.into(), v.into()))
83	            .collect();
84	        self.inner.set_footer_hint_override(Some(mapped));
85	    }
86	
87	    /// Clear any previously set custom hint items and restore the default hints.
88	    pub fn clear_hint_items(&mut self) {
89	        self.inner.set_footer_hint_override(/*items*/ None);
90	    }
91	
92	    /// Desired height (in rows) for a given width.
93	    pub fn desired_height(&self, width: u16) -> u16 {
94	        self.inner.desired_height(width)
95	    }
96	
97	    /// Compute the on-screen cursor position for the given area.
98	    pub fn cursor_pos(&self, area: Rect) -> Option<(u16, u16)> {
99	        self.inner.cursor_pos(area)
100	    }
101	
102	    /// Render the input into the provided buffer at `area`.
103	    pub fn render_ref(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
104	        self.inner.render(area, buf);
105	    }
106	
107	    /// Return true if a paste-burst detection is currently active.
108	    pub fn is_in_paste_burst(&self) -> bool {
109	        self.inner.is_in_paste_burst()
110	    }
111	
112	    /// Flush a pending paste-burst if the inter-key timeout has elapsed.
113	    /// Returns true if text changed and a redraw is warranted.
114	    pub fn flush_paste_burst_if_due(&mut self) -> bool {
115	        let flushed = self.inner.flush_paste_burst_if_due();
116	        self.drain_app_events();
117	        flushed
118	    }
119	
120	    /// Recommended delay to schedule the next micro-flush frame while a
121	    /// paste-burst is active.
122	    pub fn recommended_flush_delay() -> Duration {
123	        crate::bottom_pane::ChatComposer::recommended_paste_flush_delay()
124	    }
125	
126	    fn drain_app_events(&mut self) {
127	        while self.rx.try_recv().is_ok() {}
128	    }
129	}
130	
131	impl Default for ComposerInput {
132	    fn default() -> Self {
133	        Self::new()
134	    }
135	}
```

#### codex-rs/app-server/src/command_exec.rs — calls(calls), instantiates(instantiates), references(references), start(method)

```rust
140	}
141	
142	impl CommandExecManager {
143	    pub(crate) async fn start(
144	        &self,
145	        params: StartCommandExecParams,
146	    ) -> Result<(), JSONRPCErrorError> {
147	        let StartCommandExecParams {
148	            outgoing,
149	            request_id,
150	            process_id,
151	            exec_request,
152	            started_network_proxy,
153	            tty,
154	            stream_stdin,
155	            stream_stdout_stderr,
156	            output_bytes_cap,
157	            size,
158	        } = params;
159	        if process_id.is_none() && (tty || stream_stdin || stream_stdout_stderr) {
160	            return Err(invalid_request(
161	                "command/exec tty or streaming requires a client-supplied processId",
162	            ));
163	        }
164	        let process_id = process_id.map_or_else(
165	            || {
166	                InternalProcessId::Generated(
167	                    self.next_generated_process_id
168	                        .fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed),
169	                )
170	            },
171	            InternalProcessId::Client,
172	        );
173	        let process_key = ConnectionProcessId {
174	            connection_id: request_id.connection_id,
175	            process_id: process_id.clone(),
176	        };
177	
178	        if matches!(exec_request.sandbox, SandboxType::WindowsRestrictedToken) {
179	            if tty || stream_stdin || stream_stdout_stderr {
180	                return Err(invalid_request(
181	                    "streaming command/exec is not supported with windows sandbox",
182	                ));
183	            }
184	            if output_bytes_cap != Some(DEFAULT_OUTPUT_BYTES_CAP) {
185	                return Err(invalid_request(
186	                    "custom outputBytesCap is not supported with windows sandbox",
187	                ));
188	            }
189	            if let InternalProcessId::Client(_) = &process_id {
190	                let mut sessions = self.sessions.lock().await;
191	                if sessions.contains_key(&process_key) {
192	                    return Err(invalid_request(format!(
193	                        "duplicate active command/exec process id: {}",
194	                        process_key.process_id.error_repr(),
195	                    )));
196	                }
197	                sessions.insert(
198	                    process_key.clone(),
199	                    CommandExecSession::UnsupportedWindowsSandbox,
200	                );
201	            }
202	            let sessions = Arc::clone(&self.sessions);
203	            tokio::spawn(async move {
204	                let _started_network_proxy = started_network_proxy;
205	                match codex_core::sandboxing::execute_env(exec_request, /*stdout_stream*/ None)
206	                    .await
207	                {
208	                    Ok(output) => {
209	                        outgoing
210	                            .send_response(
211	                                request_id,
212	                                CommandExecResponse {
213	                                    exit_code: output.exit_code,
214	                                    stdout: output.stdout.text,
215	                                    stderr: output.stderr.text,
216	                                },
217	                            )
218	                            .await;
219	                    }
220	                    Err(err) => {
221	                        outgoing
222	                            .send_error(request_id, internal_error(format!("exec failed: {err}")))
223	                            .await;
224	                    }
225	                }
226	                sessions.lock().await.remove(&process_key);
227	            });
228	            return Ok(());
229	        }
230	
231	        let ExecRequest {
232	            command,
233	            cwd,
234	            env,
235	            expiration,
236	            sandbox: _sandbox,
237	            arg0,
238	            ..
239	        } = exec_request;
240	        // TODO(anp): Keep PathUri through the local command launch boundary.
241	        let cwd = cwd
242	            .to_abs_path()
243	            .map_err(|err| invalid_request(format!("invalid command cwd: {err}")))?;
244	
245	        let stream_stdin = tty || stream_stdin;
246	        let stream_stdout_stderr = tty || stream_stdout_stderr;
247	        let (control_tx, control_rx) = mpsc::channel(32);
248	        let notification_process_id = match &process_id {
249	            InternalProcessId::Generated(_) => None,
250	            InternalProcessId::Client(process_id) => Some(process_id.clone()),
251	        };
252	
253	        let sessions = Arc::clone(&self.sessions);
254	        let (program, args) = command
255	            .split_first()
256	            .ok_or_else(|| invalid_request("command must not be empty"))?;
257	        {
258	            let mut sessions = self.sessions.lock().await;
259	            if sessions.contains_key(&process_key) {
260	                return Err(invalid_request(format!(
261	                    "duplicate active command/exec process id: {}",
262	                    process_key.process_id.error_repr(),
263	                )));
264	            }
265	            sessions.insert(
266	                process_key.clone(),
267	                CommandExecSession::Active { control_tx },
268	            );
269	        }
270	        let spawned = if tty {
271	            codex_utils_pty::spawn_pty_process(
272	                program,
273	                args,
274	                cwd.as_path(),
275	                &env,
276	                &arg0,
277	                size.unwrap_or_default(),
278	            )
279	            .await
280	        } else if stream_stdin {
281	            codex_utils_pty::spawn_pipe_process(program, args, cwd.as_path(), &env, &arg0).await
282	        } else {
283	            codex_utils_pty::spawn_pipe_process_no_stdin(program, args, cwd.as_path(), &env, &arg0)
284	                .await
285	        };
286	        let spawned = match spawned {
287	            Ok(spawned) => spawned,
288	            Err(err) => {
289	                self.sessions.lock().await.remove(&process_key);
290	                return Err(internal_error(format!("failed to spawn command: {err}")));
291	            }
292	        };
293	        tokio::spawn(async move {
294	            let _started_network_proxy = started_network_proxy;
295	            run_command(RunCommandParams {
296	                outgoing,
297	                request_id: request_id.clone(),
298	                process_id: notification_process_id,
299	                spawned,
300	                control_rx,
301	                stream_stdin,
302	                stream_stdout_stderr,
303	                expiration,
304	                output_bytes_cap,
305	            })
306	            .await;
307	            sessions.lock().await.remove(&process_key);
308	        });
309	        Ok(())
310	    }
311	
312	    pub(crate) async fn write(
313	        &self,
```

#### codex-rs/core/src/tools/runtimes/shell/unix_escalation.rs — calls(calls), references(references), instantiates(instantiates), clone(calls), prompt(method)

```rust
432	        }
433	    }
434	
435	    async fn prompt(
436	        &self,
437	        program: &AbsolutePathBuf,
438	        argv: &[String],
439	        workdir: &AbsolutePathBuf,
440	        stopwatch: &Stopwatch,
441	        additional_permissions: Option<AdditionalPermissionProfile>,
442	    ) -> anyhow::Result<PromptDecision> {
443	        let command = join_program_and_argv(program, argv);
444	        let workdir = workdir.clone();
445	        let session = self.session.clone();
446	        let turn = self.turn.clone();
447	        let call_id = self.call_id.clone();
448	        let approval_id = Some(Uuid::new_v4().to_string());
449	        let environment_id = Some(self.environment_id.clone());
450	        let source = self.tool_name;
451	        let guardian_review_id = routes_approval_to_guardian(&turn).then(new_guardian_review_id);
452	        Ok(stopwatch
453	            .pause_for(async move {
454	                // 1) Run PermissionRequest hooks
455	                let permission_request = PermissionRequestPayload::bash(
456	                    codex_shell_command::parse_command::shlex_join(&command),
457	                    /*description*/ None,
458	                );
459	                let effective_approval_id = approval_id.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| call_id.clone());
460	                match run_permission_request_hooks(
461	                    &session,
462	                    &turn,
463	                    &effective_approval_id,
464	                    permission_request,
465	                )
466	                .await
467	                {
468	                    Some(PermissionRequestDecision::Allow) => {
469	                        return PromptDecision {
470	                            decision: ReviewDecision::Approved,
471	                            guardian_review_id: None,
472	                            rejection_message: None,
473	                        };
474	                    }
475	                    Some(PermissionRequestDecision::Deny { message }) => {
476	                        return PromptDecision {
477	                            decision: ReviewDecision::Denied,
478	                            guardian_review_id: None,
479	                            rejection_message: Some(message),
480	                        };
481	                    }
482	                    None => {}
483	                }
484	
485	                // 2) Route to Guardian if configured
486	                if let Some(review_id) = guardian_review_id.clone() {
487	                    let decision = review_approval_request(
488	                        &session,
489	                        &turn,
490	                        review_id.clone(),
491	                        GuardianApprovalRequest::Execve {
492	                            id: call_id.clone(),
493	                            source,
494	                            program: program.to_string_lossy().into_owned(),
495	                            argv: argv.to_vec(),
496	                            cwd: workdir.clone(),
497	                            additional_permissions,
498	                        },
499	                        /*retry_reason*/ None,
500	                    )
501	                    .await;
502	                    return PromptDecision {
503	                        decision,
504	                        guardian_review_id,
505	                        rejection_message: None,
506	                    };
507	                }
508	
509	                // 3) Fall back to regular user prompt
510	                let decision = session
511	                    .request_command_approval(
512	                        &turn,
513	                        call_id,
514	                        approval_id,
515	                        environment_id,
516	                        command,
517	                        workdir.clone(),
518	                        /*reason*/ None,
519	                        /*network_approval_context*/ None,
520	                        /*proposed_execpolicy_amendment*/ None,
521	                        additional_permissions,
522	                        Some(vec![ReviewDecision::Approved, ReviewDecision::Abort]),
523	                    )
524	                    .await;
525	                PromptDecision {
526	                    decision,
```
